description:
Goal
Make `compiler2::drive_test::compiler2_interp_runs_selective_receive_with_make_ref_from_backend_artifacts` terminate under a hard timeout and either resolve or fail with a precise semantic/runtime blocker. After fz-rh2.18.9.12, the prior fast missing-return wait changed into a >120s hang, so this needs a telemetry-led diagnosis rather than another local return fallback.

Signal
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 120s cargo test --lib compiler2::drive_test::compiler2_interp_runs_selective_receive_with_make_ref_from_backend_artifacts -- --exact --nocapture` exits before timeout.
- Telemetry identifies the repeating fact/job cycle or runtime receive state responsible for the hang.
- The fix does not reintroduce missing-return-as-none/any, retained-output shims, longer timeouts, or receive-specific semantic shortcuts.

Strategy
Capture `--log-telemetry`/job telemetry for the fixture, identify the minimal repeated fact edge, and repair the fact/evidence model at the producer boundary. Treat old-world behavior as inspiration only.

Outro
No unbounded selective-receive test, no hidden timeout dependency, no special receive wait shim, and no broad any/none sentinel standing in for missing receive evidence.

comments.text: 2026-06-12 wider-suite refresh: even with compiler2_interp_runs_selective_receive_with_make_ref_from_backend_artifacts and enum_tier0_fixture_exercises_basic_operations skipped, /opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 300s cargo test --lib compiler2:: -- --test-threads=1 ... timed out while quiet at compiler2::port_interp_test::selective_receive_pinned_ref_out_of_order_server_replies. Treat the receive ticket as covering both the drive/native selective receive hang and the port-interp selective receive hang until telemetry proves separate root causes.

title: Compiler2 selective receive: stop semantic/native fixture timeout after return-readiness fixes
